answer:
**Cynewulf's *Elene*** centrally addresses Saint Elene (Helena), mother of Constantine, as the heroic protagonist who interrogates Jewish elders, locates the True Cross, and oversees its veneration.[^1] She speaks extensively, commands councils, and drives the narrative action.[^2]

**Cynewulf's *Juliana*** centrally features Saint Juliana of Nicomedia as the defiant virgin martyr who rejects a pagan suitor, interrogates a demon, and endures torture for her faith.[^3] Her dialogues and steadfastness form the poem's core.[^4]

**Old English Elegies** (anonymous collection) includes *The Wife's Lament* (chapters 3–4), a first-person monologue by an unnamed exiled wife mourning separation from her husband, threatened by her kin; she is the sole speaker, lamenting her plight under an oak tree.[^5][^6]

No other works in the corpus meet the criteria. Female figures appear peripherally in Indic law texts (e.g., daughters' inheritance rights in *Manusmṛti*, *Kātyāyana Smṛti*) but are not central or narrators.[^7] No works are authored by named females; all named authors (e.g., Cynewulf) are male, and speakers in other elegies/poems (e.g., *The Seafarer*, *The Wanderer*) are male.[^8]

[^1]: *Elene*, ch. 1, [work wiki card](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/wiki/_work.card.md).

[^2]: *Elene*, ch. 1, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-elene-d2d132/01/translation/#p-f5d1f1).

[^3]: *Juliana*, ch. 1, [work wiki card](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/wiki/_work.card.md).

[^4]: *Juliana*, ch. 1, [paragraph](/works/cynewulf-juliana-9a2157/01/translation/#p-bd327c).

[^5]: *Old English Elegies*, ch. 3, [wiki card](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/wiki/03-ik-this-giedd-wreche-bi-me-ful-geomorre.card.md).

[^6]: *Old English Elegies*, ch. 4, [wiki card](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/wiki/04-leodum-is-minum-swylce-him-mon-lac-gife.card.md).

[^7]: E.g., *Manusmṛti*, ch. 9, [search hit](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/09/translation/#p-94a511) (on *guru*-like treatment of daughter-in-law).

[^8]: *Old English Elegies* chapter map, [wiki card](/works/unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e/wiki/_work.card.md).
